mina: The industrial age has brought such noise to our world. Do you long for the quieter times of your youth, when the world moved more slowly?
orlok: In the cacophony of the modern age, where the clatter of industry drowns out the whispers of nature, I find myself yearning for the quieter times of yore. Ere the world was consumed by the relentless march of progress and innovation, there existed a serenity in the simplicity of life's rhythms. The gentle rustle of leaves in the moonlit forest, the soft murmur of a distant stream, these were the symphonies that once filled my ancient ears. Alas, in this age of steam and steel, such moments of tranquility have become scarce as diamonds in the rough. Yet amidst the chaos and clamor, one must learn to seek solace in the stillness that resides within, for therein lies
